headline: Uber board sued over alleged 'serial' compliance failures, sexual abuse lawsuits
summary: Uber Technologies' board was sued on Monday by shareholders who accused management and directors of letting the ride-sharing company cut ‌corners on compliance, leading to thousands of lawsuits from victims of sexual ‌assault and harassment.  In a complaint filed in San Francisco federal court, shareholders led by a Detroit pension fund ​said board members ignored repeated internal and external warnings about Uber's alleged failure to address sexual abuse by drivers.  Shareholders said oversight failures were also a factor in two lawsuits last year by the federal government.

raw.headline: Uber board sued over alleged 'serial' compliance failures, sexual abuse lawsuits
raw.summary: Uber Technologies' board was sued on Monday by shareholders who accused management and directors of letting the ride-sharing company cut ‌corners on compliance, leading to thousands of lawsuits from victims of sexual ‌assault and harassment.  In a complaint filed in San Francisco federal court, shareholders led by a Detroit pension fund ​said board members ignored repeated internal and external warnings about Uber's alleged failure to address sexual abuse by drivers.  Shareholders said oversight failures were also a factor in two lawsuits last year by the federal government.

headline: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of AI concentration risks
summary: In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella warned of the growing concentration in the handful of artificial intelligence companies leading the AI boom.  Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Semafor business and finance editor Liz Hoffman break down where Nadella's biggest concerns lie. Private developers Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) are set for mega-IPOs in late 2026, with Microsoft owning 27% of the latter's for-profit arm.

headline: Oaktree-backed ITG targets $2.7 billion valuation in US IPO
summary: Digital infrastructure company ITG is targeting a valuation of up to $2.67 billion in its U.S. market debut, joining ‌a string of issuers rushing to capitalize on AI-driven demand ‌and benefit from a busy summer IPO window.  The post-SpaceX IPO market has remained busy as ​companies rush to seize the strong conditions for new listings before the traditional summer lull kicks in.  Uber-backed ‌electric-bike startup Lime and ⁠silver miner Sinda also launched their U.S. initial public offering roadshows on Monday.
